evidence: TASK-037 (caller-as-reconciler conflict resolution) verified end-to-end across three sub-fixes. (1) FGAP-068 — the update conflict resolver no longer spawns a subordinate pi-bound mergetool (deleted resolve-conflicts.ts); pi-context update SURFACES the conflict set (structured under --json + a renderConflicts report with reconcile guidance on the text surface) and the calling agent reconciles. (2) FGAP-069 — a new reflected auth-gated op resolve-conflict --schemaName <name> [--schema <R>] writes the reconciled schema AND advances the merge base to the catalog body (stampBaselineFromBody), so a subsequent update converges instead of re-deriving the conflict. (3) FGAP-070 (CRITICAL) — updateContext's merge arm now stamps the merge base := the catalog (theirs) body, not the on-disk merged body, and merged is dropped from the post-loop on-disk baseline refresh; a kept-local divergence (reconciled conflict OR plain disjoint auto-merge) now persists as locally-modified and survives subsequent updates instead of being resynced to the catalog on the next run. Live CLI end-to-end: a both-diverged conflict (R=boolean, catalog=string) -> resolve-conflict --yes -> THREE live update --yes runs, notes.type stays boolean at every step, every update merged:[tasks] (no resynced flip) — the exact loop that clobbered before now a stable fixed point. build+check+full-test green (auth-gate canon 18 incl. resolve-conflict). Fresh adversarial probe PASS, no defects: own multi-update harness (4+ updates) for both the resolve-conflict and disjoint-auto-merge paths durable; base content_hash == catalog body (not merged); resynced/migrated unbroken (resync then in-sync, no re-resync loop); dryRun stamps nothing; a refused schema keeps its drift signal; mergeSchema algorithm byte-unchanged (only the recorded baseline changed); no test still asserts the buggy post-merge catalog-ahead state.

criteria_results.criterion: the update conflict path does not spawn a subordinate agent; the conflict set is returned to the calling agent (closes FGAP-068)
criteria_results.evidence: resolve-conflicts.ts deleted; the only runPiBound caller is the pi-bound command; renderConflicts on the text surface + structured conflicts under --json; live update dispatches no sub-pi

criteria_results.criterion: a calling agent can resolve a conflict so update stops re-reporting it (closes FGAP-069)
criteria_results.evidence: resolve-conflict op writes R + advances base to catalog; live resolve-conflict then update -> conflict gone, R preserved; tests i/j/k converge

criteria_results.criterion: a kept-local divergence (reconciled or disjoint-merged) survives subsequent updates and is not resynced to the catalog (closes FGAP-070)
criteria_results.evidence: merge arm stamps base := catalog body; live 3x update keeps notes.type=boolean (merged, never resynced); adversarial 4+ update harness durable for both paths; base hash == catalog not merged

criteria_results.criterion: runtime demo + adversarial probe + full gates green, no regression
criteria_results.evidence: build/check/test fail 0; auth-gate canon 18; fresh probe PASS no defects; mergeSchema byte-unchanged
